sanskrit: ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ । माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः ॥
hindiTranslation: उसके बाद सफेद घोड़ों से युक्त महान् रथ पर बैठे हुए लक्ष्मीपति भगवान् श्रीकृष्ण और पाण्डुपुत्र अर्जुन ने दिव्य शंखों को बड़े जोर से बजाया ॥
englishTranslation: Then, seated on the great chariot yoked with white horses, Madhava and Pandava blew their divine conches.
essence: Resolve answers chaos before words begin.
speaker: Sanjaya
simpleMeaning: Krishna and Arjuna stand together on their great chariot and sound their conches. It marks their answer to the Kaurava war cries.
simpleInsight: A calm, powerful response can change the whole feel of a moment.
detailedMeaning: The battlefield is already roaring with enemy conches and drums. On the opposite side, Krishna and Arjuna stand together on their white-horsed chariot and answer with their own conches. The moment turns from threat to resolve.
modernRelevance: Your phone lights up with bad news, and everyone around you is already panicking. You take one breath, steady yourself, and answer with clarity instead of fear.
translationLiteral: ततः (then) / श्वेतैः (with white) / हयैः (horses) / युक्ते (yoked) / महति (great) / स्यन्दने (on the chariot) / स्थितौ (seated) / माधवः (Madhava) / पाण्डवः (the Pandava) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / दिव्यौ (divine) / शङ्खौ (conches) / प्रदध्मतुः (blew)
